items.title: From latency to instant: Modernizing GitHub Issues navigation performance
items.creator: Natalie Guevara
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: From latency to instant: Modernizing GitHub Issues navigation performance

images.alt: Graph showing navigation mix for issues show route (57.6% hard, 37.5% react).
Graph showing HPC distribution by navigation type (2.05 hard, 1.76 turbo, 1.04 react).
Architectural diagram showing the client cache layer.
Graph showing HPC distribution after cache rollout.

Flow diagram showing preheating process. Steps are: Look at issues index, For each issue in the list trigger a preheat request, Is data in the cache present? if yes, add to IndexDB. If no, fetch data, then add to IndexDB.
Diagram showing the in-memory cache layer.
Graph showing HPC distribution after preheating rollout.
Diagram showing service worker interception flow.

items.content:
How the GitHub Issues team used client-side caching, smart prefetching, and service workers to make navigation feel instant.

The post From latency to instant: Modernizing GitHub Issues navigation performance appeared first on The GitHub Blog.

items.title: Dungeons & Desktops: 10 roguelikes that never die (because their communities won’t let them)

items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Dungeons & Desktops: 10 roguelikes that never die (because their communities won’t let them)
items.content:
Roguelikes don’t die. They fork, mutate, get argued over, rewritten, abandoned, and revived again. Sometimes all at once.

The post Dungeons & Desktops: 10 roguelikes that never die (because their communities won’t let them) appeared first on The GitHub Blog.

items.translated_title: How researchers are using GitHub Innovation Graph data to reveal the “digital complexity” of nations
items.content:

items.title: Improving token efficiency in GitHub Agentic Workflows
items.creator: Landon Cox
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Improving token efficiency in GitHub Agentic Workflows

images.alt: Graph showing token savings across Auto-Triage Issues, Daily Compiler Quality, Community Attribution, Security Guard, adn Smoke Claude.

items.content:
Agentic workflows that run on every pull request can quietly accumulate large API bills. Here's how we instrumented our own production workflows, found the inefficiencies, and built agents to fix them.

The post Improving token efficiency in GitHub Agentic Workflows appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
